text: fat is 6.9g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
carbs is 66.3g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
protein is 16.9g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
vitamin e at 3% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 44% DV, iron at 26% DV, zinc at 36% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

pros first. contains useful beta-glucans because adds cardiovascular and satiety context beyond the base nutrient display. easy to build meals around because works well as a simple staple in many eating patterns. can support digestive regularity because the fibre profile can be practically useful beyond the raw grams display.

on the weaker side. contains some anti-nutrients because that can slightly reduce mineral absorption. healthiness depends on what you add to it because sweet add-ons can change the whole picture. not ideal for everyone digestively because some people just do not tolerate it that well.
A tier. Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
